subject: LU-6401 headers: move lu_fid, ost_id funcs out of lustre_idl.h

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 1:

The general direction looks ok, but the proliferation of files to include will quickly become annoying. Can these 2 new headers be included from liblustreapi.h instead so existing applications don't have to be modified?

Patch Set 1:

This patch moves functions from a dumping-ground style header into ones that can be picked up as needed.  Not all files that include liblustreapi.h need the various fid functions, though they need some concept of what a fid is.

Only llog_reader needs the functions in lustre_ostid.h.  Only 4 .c files in the utils directory need lustre_fid.h out of the 34 that are there.  If a majority of the files needed it, I could see your argument.

Patch Set 8:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 8:

Reduce the scope of what was done in the last patch. Ran the new headers against the upstream checkpatch and fixed up the new headers to be linux kernel compliant. Now that we have move so much out of lustre_idl.h we can remove its use from various user land utilites. Now lustre_idl.h is included if only it is needed.

Patch Set 8:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 8: Code-Review-1

I didn't notice before but we now have 2 files called lustre_fid.h. That will certainly lead to some confusion.

Patch Set 8:

Its not incorrect to have two headers with the same name. One file is the uapi header and the other is what the kernel needs. This is done upstream often. What causes the confusion is that the uapi directory is located inside of the internal kernel headers directory. I plan on fixing that soon.

commit.subject: LU-6401 headers: move lu_fid, ost_id funcs out of lustre_idl.h
commit.message:
LU-6401 headers: move lu_fid, ost_id funcs out of lustre_idl.h

Move lu_fid functions into lustre/lustre_fid.h
Move ost_id functions into lustre/lustre_ostid.h
Fix indenting, include new headers as needed
